what_it_does: Defines a React function component FloorplanOverlay that renders a modal floorplan view as a percent-positioned canvas of labelled rooms plus clickable navigation dots. Rooms and dots are defined as hardcoded arrays with percent left/top/width/height (rooms) and x/y (dots). Clicking the backdrop closes the overlay; clicking a dot calls onPick(id); the dot matching activeHub gets an 'active' class. It exposes itself on window.FloorplanOverlay.
observations: rooms array: six rooms (entry, living/kitchen, bed1, bath, bed2, balcony) with percent geometry within the canvas
dots array: four navigation positions (entry, living, bed2, balcony) with percent x/y
Backdrop div has onClick={onClose}; inner card stops propagation with e.stopPropagation()
Dot button toggles 'active' class via activeHub === d.id and applies inline absolute positioning + translate(-50%,-50%) centering
Uses optional chaining onPick?.(d.id) so onPick is safe if undefined
An inline-styled paragraph gives the instruction 'Tap a position to jump there in the Hub.' using var() tokens for font/color
Close button is positioned inline (top:18,right:18) over the card
standouts: Percent-based room and dot layout makes the floorplan resolution-independent within its canvas
Active-dot highlighting tied to the externally-supplied activeHub prop
conventions: Follows the kit's window.<Component> registration and vh- class prefix
Uses design tokens via var() for the instruction text and dot border, consistent with hub.css
Backdrop-click-to-close with inner stopPropagation is a standard modal idiom
concerns: Room and dot positions are hand-tuned magic percentages with no source of truth beyond this file
Some inline styles duplicate what hub.css already defines for .vh-fp-dot (position:absolute, transform) — partial redundancy
notes: A modal floorplan with hardcoded percent-positioned rooms and clickable jump dots that report the picked room via onPick.
questions: Where do the room/dot coordinates come from for a real apartment — is there a data source, or are they always hand-authored per floorplan?
What does onPick actually navigate to in production (the parent maps it to a panel switch in this mockup)?
purpose_vs_actual: n/a (no stated purpose beyond the filename); the component does render an interactive floorplan overlay as the name implies.
apparent_themes: Spatial navigation of a property by plan view
Map-to-hub linking (dots jump to viewer positions)
Overlay/modal interaction over the main stage
intention_signals: Aims to let a buyer orient within an apartment by tapping rooms on a 2D plan to jump to the corresponding panoramic hub position.
novelty: The plan-view dots that act as spatial jump links into a separate panoramic viewer state.
contribution: Contributes a floorplan-as-navigation pattern: a percent-positioned room canvas with clickable jump dots that bind to an active position, reusable for any spatial map-to-view linking in a fused system.
summary_for_embedding: A React modal overlay component rendering an apartment floorplan as percent-positioned labelled rooms with clickable navigation dots that report the picked room and highlight the active hub position.
intention_for_embedding: Built to let a buyer navigate a property spatially by tapping rooms on a 2D plan to jump to the matching panoramic hub view.
